purpose: Compact decision map for evaluating a new service layer around custom tools, apps, and interactive products without drifting into portfolio-first positioning.

currentState.nextStrategicQuestion: Should custom tools/apps become a defined service offer?
currentState.guardrail: Any new project should be added only if it strengthens a service offer, not just to increase volume of showcased work.

serviceOptions.label: Custom Tools & App Development
serviceOptions.notes: Clear, but risks sounding commodity unless paired with a stronger positioning statement.

serviceOptions.label: Custom Tools, Apps & Interactive Products
serviceOptions.notes: Broad enough to include internal tools, client-facing apps, creator builds, and interactive brand experiences without sounding like a generic dev shop.

serviceOptions.label: Digital Products, Tools & App Builds
serviceOptions.notes: Viable if the site wants a slightly more product-oriented tone.

includeIf: the work proves a service you can actually sell now
the project shows strategic problem-solving, not just visual experimentation
the offer complements systems / website / CRM work rather than competing with them
the proof can be framed as useful to businesses, creators, or teams with specific needs
excludeIf: the project is only interesting as personal experimentation
the project cannot be tied to a buyer problem
the work would make the site feel like a portfolio-first studio instead of a services-led business

recommendedDirection.positioning: For teams, founders, and brands that need something more specialized than a standard website or off-the-shelf software.
recommendedDirection.recommendation: Plan as a specialty or next-tier service expansion, not as a replacement for the current core service system.

nextProofCandidates.notes: Strong if it demonstrates real product thinking, user flow, and app design rather than a hobby prototype.
Especially valuable because it blends website, artist platform, and interactive tool experience.
Use only if the build is mature and visually convincing enough to support a sellable offer.
Better fit for website-strategy / trust-led client work than custom tools/apps.

suggestedNextSteps: Define the service offer before adding more work entries.
Identify which proof candidates are strong enough to support the offer now.
Only then build the service page or service-expansion surface.
